name: requests
description: HTTP client for REST APIs and web services. On some ESP32 firmware builds (custom-compiled), requests is frozen into the firmware and available without installation. On standard MicroPython builds, install via mip: 'mip.install("requests")'. DO NOT use 'urequests'.
differences_from_cpython: MicroPython implementation. No sessions, no streaming upload. Supports HTTP and HTTPS.
kwargs: json: dict to JSON-encode as body
auth: (user, pass) tuple for Basic Auth

methods.name: Response.close
methods.description: Close the response and release the socket.

example:
import requests

r = requests.get('https://httpbin.org/get')
print(r.status_code)
print(r.json())

r = requests.post('https://httpbin.org/post', json={'key': 'value'})
print(r.text)

availability.standard_firmware: NOT built-in. Install via: import mip; mip.install('requests')
availability.custom_firmware: May be frozen into firmware. Check with: help('modules') | grep requests
availability.this_device_verified: Frozen into firmware (custom build with requests, urequests, umqtt, websocket, aioespnow, etc.)

esp32_notes: HTTPS requires TLS support (memory: ~30KB for handshake).
Large responses may cause MemoryError. Use streaming if available.
attributes_note: Response.headers and Response.status_code are instance attributes (not in dir()), accessible after making a request.
